title: God's Voice or Anxiety? 5 Biblical Tests
hook: Is that God's voice - or your anxiety talking? Try these 5 quick tests.
script: Is that God's voice - or your anxiety talking? Try these 5 quick tests. One: Does it agree with Scripture? God won't contradict His Word; start with 2 Timothy 3:16. Two: Does it sound like your Shepherd? John 10:27 - Jesus leads, He doesn't accuse. Three: What fruit will it grow? Galatians 5: love, joy, peace, not panic or pride. Four: Do you sense Christ's peace guarding your heart? Colossians 3:15, not frantic pressure. Five: Do wise believers affirm it? Proverbs 11:14. Psychology note: intrusive thoughts feel urgent, absolute, and fear-based. Try this 60-second check: breathe, read a verse, ask about fruit, write it down, text a mentor, wait for peace. Jesus, I surrender this decision. Speak - your servant is listening.

title: When God Feels Silent: Discern in 60 Seconds
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this - His voice is clearer than you think.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this - His voice is clearer than you think. Ask these five things. Does this thought match the Bible's counsel? 2 Timothy 3:16. Does it carry the Shepherd's tone - firm yet kind? John 10:27. Does it point you toward love, joy, and self-control? Galatians 5. Does it settle you with Christ's peace instead of a countdown clock? Colossians 3:15. Do trusted believers confirm it? Proverbs 11:14. Psych insight: confirmation bias and catastrophizing make anxiety sound holy. Quick practice: pause, name the thought, hold it beside Scripture, check fruit and peace, message a mentor, wait 24 hours. Pray, Lord, align my steps with Your voice, not my fears.

title: Stop Overthinking: Know It's God's Voice
hook: You need to hear this today: God's voice won't rush or shame you.
script: You need to hear this today: God's voice won't rush or shame you. Picture the decision that's stressing you. Now run it through these filters. God never contradicts Scripture - 2 Timothy 3:16. His sheep know His tone - John 10:27 - truthful, gentle, not condemning. His leading grows the fruit of the Spirit - Galatians 5 - love and patience, not ego. His peace acts like an umpire - Colossians 3:15 - no frantic panic. And wise counsel brings clarity - Proverbs 11:14. Your anxious brain shouts with urgency, all-or-nothing, worst-case stories; the Spirit guides with steady light. One-minute reset: inhale, pray, open a Psalm, journal one sentence, text a mature friend. Father, I choose Your pace. Close the wrong doors. I'll wait.
